text:
The boarding passes said Lisbon. Anthony had booked the flights forty minutes before they left the house, which was how he always did it, which was why Luisana kept a go-bag packed at all times, which was why Aaliyah had developed a stress response to the sound of her dad refreshing airline apps at weird hours.

"It was a great deal," Anthony said, for the fourth time since the taxi. He had the look of a man who knew he was not going to stop saying this.

Elijah was asleep across both their laps before the plane reached cruising altitude. Sully, improbably cleared for the cabin, was also asleep, curled into Elijah's armpit like a small pastry. Aaliyah had one earbud in and was watching the flight tracker the way other people watched thrillers.

The screen glitched. Just the little plane icon. It stuttered, reversed three seconds of its path, then continued forward as though nothing had happened.

"Dad," she said. "Did you see that?"

Anthony looked at the screen, which was now perfectly normal. Aaliyah opened her notebook to a fresh page and drew a small, precise arrow pointing left. Below it, she wrote: it went backward.
